message:
The other day, I was in the kitchen whipping up dinner when I noticed my smoke alarm blinking like it was trying to start a dance party. I had put off changing the batteries for ages, thinking, "It’s fine, it hasn’t gone off yet!" But then I remembered the last time I had a similar thought—when my brother-in-law casually mentioned how his alarm went off during a little cooking mishap. Cue my inner smugness. I couldn’t help but think, "I told you so!" 

So there I was, feeling rather clever, as I grabbed the ladder and replaced the batteries on both alarms. It was a small chore, but it felt like a little win. It’s funny how we often think we’re invincible until life reminds us otherwise. 

A few minutes later, I even reviewed our family escape plan, just in case. You know, I’d rather be prepared than smug! This little refresher helped, but I’m curious—what’s your go-to tip for keeping your home safe from fire? 🔥
